howItWorks.intro: This required minimum distribution calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.

rules.name: RMD Formula
rules.formula: RMD = Account Balance / Life Expectancy Divisor
rules.explanation: Divide your prior year-end balance by the IRS Uniform Lifetime Table factor for your age.

howItWorks.outro: Tax laws and financial markets change frequently. Verify current rates with your financial institution.

faq.question: When do RMDs start?
faq.answer: Under SECURE 2.0 Act, RMDs begin at age 73 for those born 1951-1959, and age 75 for those born 1960 or later. Roth IRAs do not require RMDs during the owner's lifetime.

faq.question: What happens if I miss an RMD?
faq.answer: The penalty for missing an RMD was reduced from 50% to 25% of the shortfall under SECURE 2.0. If corrected within 2 years, the penalty drops to 10%. Always take your full RMD by December 31.

faq.question: Is the Required Minimum Distribution Calculator free to use?
faq.answer: Yes, completely free with no signup required. Use it as many times as you need — there are no limits or hidden fees.

faq.question: How accurate is this calculator?
faq.answer: This calculator uses standard financial planning formulas trusted by individuals and families. Results are reliable estimates for planning purposes. For critical decisions, we recommend consulting a qualified professional to verify.

faq.question: What account balance should I enter?
faq.answer: Enter the most accurate account balance value you have available. If you're estimating, use a conservative figure. You can always run the calculator again with different values to see how changes affect the results.

sections.title: What Is the Required Minimum Distribution Calculator?
sections.body: The Required Minimum Distribution Calculator is a free online tool designed for individuals and families who need quick, accurate calculations in the financial planning space. By entering your account balance, your age this year, spouse 10+ years younger ?, you get instant results including required minimum distribution, life expectancy divisor, withdrawal percentage. No formulas to memorize, no spreadsheets to build — just enter your numbers and get the answer in seconds. Whether you're a beginner or experienced professional, this calculator saves you time and eliminates guesswork.

sections.title: Why This Calculation Matters
sections.body: Getting required minimum distribution right can make the difference between success and costly mistakes. In financial planning, small errors compound quickly. Manual calculations are error-prone and time-consuming, especially under pressure. This calculator applies proven formulas used by individuals and families worldwide, giving you confidence that your numbers are correct. Use it to manage your finances with precision and avoid common pitfalls that trip up beginners.

sections.title: When Should You Use This Calculator?
sections.body: This tool is most useful when you know your account balance and need to find the right required minimum distribution. It's also great for quick estimates before committing to a decision, and to double-check manual calculations or professional quotes, and when comparing different scenarios side by side. Bookmark this page and come back whenever you need a fast, reliable answer — the calculator is always free and requires no signup.

tips: Double-check your account balance before calculating — even small input errors can significantly change your results.
Run the calculator with different values to compare scenarios and find the optimal approach for your situation.
